steps.title: Find the slope using the difference condition
steps.content: The condition $g(x+1) - g(x) = 2$ reveals the slope of the linear function. For any linear function $g(x) = mx + b$: $$g(x+1) - g(x) = m(x+1) + b - (mx + b) = m$$ Since this difference equals 2, the slope is $m = 2$, giving us $g(x) = 2x + b$.

steps.title: Use the point condition to find the y-intercept
steps.content: Apply $g(9) = 11$ to find the remaining constant: $$g(9) = 2(9) + b = 11$$ $$18 + b = 11$$ $$b = -7$$ Therefore $g(x) = 2x - 7$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
